name: Vaibhav Sharma
title: Backend / Systems Engineer
tagline: I design reliable backend systems and production-grade architecture.
summary: Systems-oriented engineer with a track record of shipping production backend systems under real-world constraints — from constraint-based scheduling engines to database migration projects. I work across the full backend stack: API design, schema modeling, service architecture, and infrastructure automation.
philosophy: Good systems are invisible. They handle edge cases silently, scale without drama, and let teams move fast without breaking things. I build for maintainability first — not for demos.

bullets: Architecting production-grade backend systems for university and enterprise clients.
Designing constraint-based automation engines replacing manual administrative workflows.
Delivering full-stack API layers with Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, and cloud deployment.
Built timetable scheduling engine handling 40+ sections and 200+ course-faculty assignments.
Migrated legacy Firestore-based system to PostgreSQL with Spring Security and JWT auth.
Implemented rule-based student permission system reducing processing time by 80%.
Developed static code analysis tool for dependency graph modeling and fragility scoring.
Built AST-driven pipeline identifying high-change-risk modules in large codebases.
